description: To 'sethoni' is to pass a bounded piece of work, care, or responsibility from one participant to another at a transition. A handoff can include records and unresolved needs, but the receiving person still has to acknowledge what arrived and accept any responsibility. General 'sekaru' (transfer) moves something between holders or places; a handoff centres continuity between stages.
articulatory_notes: Send unvoiced air through a narrow groove along the tongue for 's', then move into true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', pass air along it, and round the lips into stressed true-mid back 'o'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: With the work or care as its object, 'sethoni' uses 'wei' to introduce the recipient. Noun 'sethoni' names a handoff. In care, unanswered questions travel with the source record; passing them on proves neither understanding nor acceptance.

examples.phi: mia wei thia ha riporu sethoni.
examples.translation: I hand the task off to you.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Distributed maintenance depends on transitions that keep unfinished work and practical knowledge visible.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A handoff does not quietly assign obligation: receipt and acceptance remain further acts.

semantic_domains.activity: transferring a bounded stage of work or care
semantic_domains.community: a transition between participants without implied acceptance
semantic_domains.temporal: movement from one stage or custodian to the next
